focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rayette seeks validation from Bobby, who remains emotionally detached; she responds by physically reconnecting, highlighting her insecurity and their strained, unbalanced relationship.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bobby actively resists Rayette's attempts to connect, rejecting her singing and physical affection through sarcasm, physical avoidance, and dismissive behavior.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: avoid emotional intimacy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: maintains emotional distance in relationship
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Rayette's need for validation unmet
evidence_quotes: He picks up one of the couch pillows and holds it over his ear.
As she moves her lips from his ear to his neck, he lifts the can to his mouth and drinks.
BOBBY You're not going to play it again.
BOBBY Why'nt you take 'at sign off your tit, Ray, an' let's go on out.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I shut her out with the pillow

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby, dazed, learns Elton was arrested for robbery and jumping bail; Elton asks Bobby to tell Stoney to get him before being driven away, leaving Bobby dejected.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: climactic rupture with work and peer
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bobby reacts with anger to Elton's accusation about abandoning a woman, throws his sandwich, and quits his job after a confrontation escalates.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: rejecting accountability and emotional exposure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally volatile and unmoored
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: avoidance of responsibility toward women and past relationships
evidence_quotes: Bobby throws his sandwich onto the rig floor near Elton's leg.
BOBBY (attacking the messenger) It's ridiculous! I'm sitting here, listening to some asshole cracker compare his life to mine!
BOBBY I hope that's all you gotta say, El, 'cause I'm about as tired of your mouth as I am workin' this stinkin' hole!
BOBBY Hey, Longcipher! I'm quitting!

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I don't owe anyone an explanation for walking away

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby and Tita share an emotional goodbye before his trip to visit her ailing father, possibly extending to Canada, with Tita expressing relief and concern as he departs.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tita informs Bobby that their father has had two strokes and is very ill; Bobby resists the news, initially refusing to travel with her but eventually acknowledging he should visit.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: avoid confronting family illness and responsibility
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bobby agrees to visit his father reluctantly, though he avoids immediate commitment and maintains emotional detachment
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: fear of returning to his family and confronting his past
evidence_quotes: Daddy's very ill.
He's had two strokes.
Don't tell me about this...
No...

relation_updates: strained interaction with sister over father's condition
status_updates: learns father has had two strokes
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I stood there and said no when she asked me to go with her

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby finds Tita with an elderly man in a wheelchair; she turns him around, revealing Nicholas, who appears unresponsive. Bobby realizes Nicholas doesn't recognize him.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bobby discovers his father Nicholas in a wheelchair, unresponsive and unable to recognize him, marking the collapse of any possibility for mutual understanding or closure.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking connection or recognition from his father
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: disillusioned and emotionally exposed
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether he still desires his father's approval
evidence_quotes: She looks up and, seeing Bobby, smiles happily, then reaches down and turns the wheelchair around.
Nicholas opens his eyes slightly and gazes through Bobby into an absolute elsewhere.
BOBBY He doesn't even know who the hell I am.

status_updates: confronts his father's physical and mental deterioration
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I stood there and he didn't even know me

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby admits he played a simple childhood piece without emotion; Catherine feels she supplied the feeling, calls him a fake, and leaves, leaving Bobby dejected.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bobby admits he played a simple piece without emotion, leading Catherine to feel deceived and call him a fake before leaving, marking a moment of personal exposure.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to reconnect through music
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: estranged from Catherine and confronted with his emotional detachment from his musical talent
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to reconcile his musical ability with his lack of authentic feeling
evidence_quotes: I picked the easiest piece I could remember. I think I first played it when I was 8 years old and I played it better then.
I didn't have any.
Then I must have been supplying it.

status_updates: Bobby exposed as emotionally detached from his talent
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I played the easiest piece I could remember, and I didn't feel a thing

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby, overwhelmed, apologizes to his unresponsive father, acknowledging their strained relationship and his own inadequacy in connecting, while reflecting on Tita's suggestion of reconciliation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: son confronting his unresponsive father in a moment of emotional need
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bobby attempts to communicate with his silent father, expresses regret over their estranged relationship, and offers an apology, showing emotional vulnerability and self-doubt.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking reconciliation despite believing it is impossible
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally overwhelmed and without resolution
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: father remains unresponsive, no mutual communication or understanding is achieved
evidence_quotes: The best I can do, is apologize.
I'm sorry it didn't work out.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I'm sorry it didn't work out

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby works on a drilling crew at Signal Hill, performing strenuous tasks like welding, lifting pipes, and operating machinery alongside fellow workers.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: establishing Bobby's current life as a manual laborer, rejecting his musical past
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bobby performs physically demanding labor on an oil rig, including operating heavy machinery, welding, and handling pipes alongside other crew members.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bobby is fully immersed in a blue-collar work environment, defined by strenuous physical tasks and camaraderie with fellow oil field workers.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether this life is self-chosen or an escape from failure
evidence_quotes: revealing Bobby in hard-hat and heavy gloves, operating the levers
The toothed bucket of a back hoe trenches into the earth, then lifts up into the air
deposits a load of earth into the rear of a truck

relation_updates: aligned with working-class peers through shared labor
status_updates: occupies role of oil field worker at Signal Hill
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I run the backhoe, weld pipes, and climb rigs—this is what I do now.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 139、INT. ELTON AND STONEY'S MOBILE HOME - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elton jokes about Bobby getting a baby, prompting Bobby to sarcastically reply he should also live in a trailer park and lose his front teeth, stretching as he stands.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bobby responds with sarcasm to Elton’s suggestion that he get a baby, mocking the idea of embracing a trailer-park family life.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reject domestic normalcy and class identity
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: reinforces emotional detachment from Rayette and their potential future
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: fear of entrapment in working-class domesticity
evidence_quotes: You know, you oughta get you one them things, Bob.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I said I should live in a trailer park with my teeth knocked out — that’s what they.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby and Tita share an emotional goodbye before his visit, with Tita expressing relief and hope for his time with her father, as Bobby abruptly leaves.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tita informs Bobby that their father has suffered two strokes and is seriously ill, leading to Bobby's visible distress and reluctance to engage with the situation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: avoids emotional engagement with family crisis
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally overwhelmed and resistant to confronting family responsibilities
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Conflict between escape and obligation to family
evidence_quotes: TITA Daddy's very ill.
TITA He's had two strokes.
BOBBY (standing up) Don't tell me about this...
He moves aimlessly about trying to subdue his anguish.

relation_updates: Tita appeals to Bobby's familial duty
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Tita crying, telling me about Dad's strokes

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby leaves to visit his sick father as Rayette stays behind, suggesting activities like getting her hair done or tanning, and they exchange subdued goodbyes.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bobby decides to travel alone to see his sick father, leaving Rayette behind at the motel and giving her money while dismissing her desire to accompany him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to assess the situation with his father before involving Rayette
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bobby has left for his family home while Rayette remains at the motel, awaiting his return
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension between Bobby's current relationship and his estranged family background
evidence_quotes: I have to see how things are first. My father's sick, you understand? They wouldn't be prepared for me bringing anyone.
He holds out a roll of bills and as she takes them:
So how long am I supposed to sit an' twiddle my thumbs in this place?
Bobby, don't talk like that...

relation_updates: instructs Rayette to wait, asserting authority
status_updates: provides financial support while enforcing separation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I have to see how things are first

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Catherine and Bobby discuss activities like fishing and concerts; she feels silly listing them, he admits nothing to do, finds it boring, and asks for her suggestions as she heads toward the door.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bobby explicitly states he has nothing to do at home and finds it boring, showing disengagement from his surroundings.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeks distraction or engagement
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: acknowledges personal boredom and lack of activity
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: lack of meaningful activity or direction
evidence_quotes: Nothing.
That's right. Have you anything to suggest?
Besides piano and swimming, what else do you do?

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I have nothing to do here

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby pleads to talk, but Catherine insists she must end things, citing unfairness to Carl; she apologizes for confusion, promises to meet later, and drives onto the ferry.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bobby tries to speak with Catherine, but she refuses and ends their affair, stating she has not been fair to Carl.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to talk to Catherine and explain himself
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally rebuffed, left alone as Catherine drives onto the ferry
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: emotional isolation and lack of closure after rejection
evidence_quotes: BOBBY I have to talk to you.
CATHERINE No, you don't have to, it isn't necessary...
She pulls ahead and moves onto the ferry.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I tried to stop her, but she wouldn't listen

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby feels uncomfortable after Catherine and Carl leave; he's left alone with Nicholas, who shows no interest, intensifying the emotional distance and tension between them.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine engages Bobby in direct conversation during a family dinner, asking about his time away and current activities, and is acknowledged by other family members as part of the household.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Catherine is present and participating in the family meal, treated as a recognized member of the household by engaging in and initiating conversation with family members.
evidence_quotes: CATHERINE (to Bobby) How long have you been...
CATHERINE I was just going to ask Robert how long he's been away from here.
CATHERINE What have you been doing since then?
BOBBY (to Catherine) How long have you been staying here?

status_updates: treated as a guest or quasi-family member
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I sit at the table, asking Bobby about his life — I belong here.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Catherine plans to relax in a hot tub and read music, then mentions she's free the day after tomorrow, when Carl has hydrotherapy. Bobby confirms he'll likely be free then.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: establishes Catherine's limited availability due to structured routine
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine indicates her schedule is tightly controlled by practice and Carl's appointments, with only the day after tomorrow open because Carl has hydrotherapy.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: managing personal time within a rigid artistic regimen
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Catherine suggests a possible meeting the day after tomorrow, contingent on Bobby's availability and Carl being occupied
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: her personal time is dependent on Carl's schedule
evidence_quotes: Tomorrow's a full practice day...
But the day after is kind of open.
Carl has hydrotherapy on Tuesdays.
If you're free.

relation_updates: dependence on Carl's schedule for personal freedom
status_updates: bound by a disciplined daily routine
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I have to wait for Carl's hydrotherapy day to find time for myself

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Carl complains about neck pain from playing violin, stops Bobby in the hallway; Catherine briefly appears before closing her door as Bobby tries to reach her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: observer and passive participant in hallway encounter
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine briefly acknowledges Bobby's presence by glancing out of her room but immediately closes the door, avoiding interaction.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: deliberately disengaged from Bobby
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: unspoken connection with Bobby suppressed by loyalty to Carl
evidence_quotes: Catherine as she glances out at them
then closes the door to her room.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I saw them in the hall, but closed the door

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby admits he played a simple childhood piece without emotion; Catherine feels she supplied the feeling, calls him a fake, and leaves, leaving Bobby dejected.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine confronts Bobby after he admits he played without emotion, leading her to feel she had projected her own feelings onto his performance and ultimately calling him a fake.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to emotionally connect through music
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: withdraws trust and affection, leaves the room
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Bobby can express authentic emotion
evidence_quotes: I was really very moved by the way you...
I picked the easiest piece I could remember. I think I first played it when I was 8 years old and I played it better then.
I didn't have any.
Then I must have been supplying it.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I thought he meant it, but he was just pretending

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Catherine and Bobby argue about sincerity and attraction; their tension escalates into a passionate, genuine kiss, marking a turning point in their relationship.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After a tense argument about sincerity and emotional avoidance, Catherine physically responds to Bobby's kiss by pulling him onto the bed and fervently returning it, marking a shift from resistance to engaged intimacy.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to maintain emotional boundaries
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: lowers her emotional defenses and engages in a passionate, mutual kiss with Bobby
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to balance directness and vulnerability in her relationship with Bobby
evidence_quotes: I'd like you to leave now. Is that direct enough?

relation_updates: shifts from conflict to intimate connection with Bobby
auxiliary.persona_anchor: He pushed me, but I finally let go

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby pleads to talk, but Catherine insists she must end things with Carl and leaves on the ferry, promising to see him later.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine ends the interaction by stating she hasn't been fair to Carl, refuses to continue the conversation with Bobby, and drives onto the ferry.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: prioritizing commitment to Carl over relationship with Bobby
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Catherine physically separates from Bobby and ends the immediate emotional exchange
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Bobby's need to explain remains unmet
evidence_quotes: I haven't been being fair to Carl. I have to tell you that.
I'm sorry everything's been so confusing, but I have to go, Robert...
Please, I'll see you later this evening.
She pulls ahead and moves onto the ferry.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I have to be fair to Carl

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby, hurt by Catherine's decision, questions her choice to live in the rest home; she affirms it, apologizes, and leaves; he quietly accepts with 'Okay.'
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine ends her romantic relationship with Bobby, explaining that his lack of self-respect and emotional grounding makes a relationship unworkable.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to maintain emotional boundaries and affirm her life choices
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Catherine has clearly rejected Bobby's advances and affirmed her decision to remain at the rest home, marking emotional closure on their relationship.
evidence_quotes: No. It's useless, Robert. It wouldn't work, not ever...
I'm trying to be delicate with you, but you're not understanding me. It's not just because of Carl, or my music, but because of you...
Yes.

relation_updates: romantic relationship terminated with Bobby Dupea
status_updates: emotionally resolved in her decision to stay at the rest home
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I had to tell him the truth — it wouldn't work because he doesn't love himself.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby feels uncomfortable after Catherine and Carl leave; he and Nicholas remain in silence, with Nicholas showing no interest in Bobby as Spicer clears the table.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: object of attention and subtle exchange
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine is seen looking at Bobby, then quickly shifts her gaze to Carl when noticed, creating a brief moment of mutual awareness.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Catherine becomes a quietly noticeable presence to Bobby during an otherwise isolating moment.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: her feelings or intentions toward Bobby remain unclear
evidence_quotes: Bobby looks at Catherine to find her looking at him.
She quickly alters her gaze to Carl.
CATHERINE I wouldn't mind doing a little work, if you're finished, and not too tired...
CATHERINE Excuse me, I don't want to hear this...

relation_updates: shared moment of eye contact with Bobby
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I looked at him, then looked away—just in time.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Catherine plans to relax in a hot tub and read music, then mentions she's free the day after tomorrow, when Carl has hydrotherapy. Bobby confirms he'll likely be free then.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: scheduling a future interaction with Bobby
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine indicates she is free the day after tomorrow, when Carl has hydrotherapy, and suggests Bobby could meet her if he is available.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: coordinating availability to spend time with Bobby
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bobby agrees he will likely be free the day after tomorrow
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about whether the meeting will happen and what it will involve
evidence_quotes: But the day after is kind of open.
Carl has hydrotherapy on Tuesdays.
If you're free.
Yeah, I'll probably be free.

relation_updates: establishes potential for future one-on-one time with Bobby
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I told him I was free Tuesday, when Carl has hydrotherapy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby admits he played a simple childhood piece without emotion; Catherine feels she supplied the feeling, calls him a fake, and leaves, leaving Bobby dejected.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine is initially moved by Bobby's piano playing, but feels deceived when he reveals he felt no emotion and played a simple piece, leading her to accuse him of being a fake.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking authenticity in emotional connection
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Catherine's belief in the emotional sincerity of Bobby's performance collapses.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: conflict between appearance and authenticity in relationships
evidence_quotes: CATHERINE That was beautiful, Robert. I'm surprised...
CATHERINE It doesn't matter. It was the feeling I was affected by.
BOBBY None.
CATHERINE Then I must have been supplying it.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I thought I felt something real in his music—until he told me he felt nothing.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Catherine credits Carl for restoring her confidence, then tentatively reconnects with Bobby, arranging to spend time together before Carl returns, to which Bobby agrees.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine acknowledges Carl's role in rebuilding her self-esteem and initiates a private meeting with Bobby, showing her emotional reconnection with him while still committed to Carl.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reconnect with Bobby while maintaining relationship with Carl
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: plans a private meeting with Bobby before Carl returns, indicating divided emotional loyalty
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to balance her feelings for Bobby with her commitment to Carl
evidence_quotes: Carl restored my confidence. He really did. He's much more substantial than you give him credit for.
She reaches over, tentatively caressing his face, then:
Do you think you could discreetly move across the hall now?
I could spend some time with you tomorrow morning, before Carl comes back, I mean, if you'd like to.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I left Joseph because he made me feel small — now I’m choosing differently

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby pleads to talk, but Catherine insists she must end things, citing unfairness to Carl; she apologizes for confusion, promises to meet later, and drives onto the ferry.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Catherine ends her romantic involvement with Bobby during a chance encounter at the ferry, stating it is unfair to Carl and affirming her need to be honest with him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: end the affair and act with integrity toward Carl
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Catherine resolves to stop seeing Bobby and reaffirms her commitment to her relationship with Carl
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether she can rebuild trust and return fully to her life with Carl after the affair
evidence_quotes: I haven't been being fair to Carl. I have to tell you that.
I'm sorry everything's been so confusing, but I have to go, Robert...
Please, I'll see you later this evening.
She pulls ahead and moves onto the ferry.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I haven't been being fair to Carl. I have to tell you that.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rayette seeks validation from Bobby, who remains emotionally detached; she responds by physically reconnecting, highlighting her insecurity and their strained, unbalanced relationship.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional validation seeker in strained relationship
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette directly expresses her emotional dependence on Bobby and pleads for his engagement with her aspirations, but is met with dismissal.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to be loved and validated by Bobby
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: remains emotionally dependent and physically present despite Bobby's rejection of her singing and emotional overtures
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Bobby refuses to listen to her singing or support her personal ambitions
evidence_quotes: I'm just tryin' to get you to take an interest in my kind a things, an' what I'm trying to do with myself...
You know, there id'n anything in the world I wouldn't do for you, baby. I started livin' the day I found you, you know that?
But you think you would? No, you're too damn selfish...

relation_updates: Bobby dismisses her singing and emotional needs
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I started livin' the day I found you

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby prepares to leave as Rayette cries silently; he expresses regret, promises to send money and call, then says goodbye, met with her sob.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette remains silent and turned away as Bobby packs and announces his departure, weeping soundlessly while he speaks and letting out a sob only after he says goodbye.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: desire to be acknowledged or kept
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally devastated and left behind, with no verbal or physical engagement as Bobby leaves
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: lack of closure or commitment from Bobby
evidence_quotes: She is still faced away from him, her shoulders trembling as she weeps soundlessly.
An audible sob finally comes from her.
BOBBY (CONT'D) I'll send you some money, that's all I can do.
BOBBY (CONT'D) And... I'll call you, like I said... (he pauses, then) Bye, Ray.

relation_updates: relationship effectively ends with Bobby's departure
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I didn’t turn around, but I felt him watching me cry

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 49、INT. BOBBY'S CAR - BIG SUR COUNTRY - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rayette sings about love and reunion as Bobby notices an overturned car on the highway with two figures arguing; she wonders if it's an accident.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette performs a song expressing her belief that Bobby will return to her, reflecting her emotional investment in their relationship.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: desire for lasting connection with Bobby
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: affirms her commitment and expectation that Bobby will return despite difficulties
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about Bobby's long-term commitment
evidence_quotes: RAYETTE (resumes singing) "You'll be burned out/Or smoked out/An' come back to me, I know..."
RAYETTE (CONT'D) You like it?
BOBBY I love it.

relation_updates: uses song to express emotional investment in relationship
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I sang him that song about fires and trails, like I knew he’d come back

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Palm and Rayette's argument escalates as Bobby interrupts, shouting for everyone to be quiet, intensifying the tension among the group.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: participant in escalating argument, asserting working-class knowledge
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette challenges Palm's claim about truck drivers by citing her experience as a waitress, asserting that salesmen and cops know the best roadside stops, which escalates their conflict.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to assert her knowledge and social identity
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally charged and defensive of her identity
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: her value is tied to being heard and recognized

I wouldn't be a waitress. They're nasty and full of crap.
You better hold onto your tongue!
Don't call me \"honey,\" mack.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby leaves to visit his sick father as Rayette stays behind, suggesting activities like getting her hair done or tanning, and they exchange subdued goodbyes.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: focal character left behind during partner's family visit
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette is excluded from Bobby's family visit and given money to stay at the motel, highlighting her financial dependence and emotional uncertainty.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to stay connected to Bobby while waiting at the motel
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: left alone at the motel with no clear timeline for Bobby's return

So how long am I supposed to sit an' twiddle my thumbs in this place?
If you can't do what I ask, Ray, use that money to go back home, then.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bobby and Rayette depart after a farewell; Rayette takes photos and extends an invitation before they leave, as Tita watches the car drive away.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette takes photos, thanks the hosts, and extends an invitation to visit, acting independently despite Bobby's eagerness to leave.

She pulls free of him and, as she moves back toward Tita

relation_updates: offers inclusive gesture to Tita and Carl's family
status_updates: shifts from passive to active role in departure ritual
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I never got the chance to thank you all for your hospitality. You tell Carl for me, if.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rayette pleads with Bobby to be good to her, asserting no one else will care for him as she does, and asks if he heard her, but he remains unresponsive.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette confronts Bobby after he pushes her away, expressing anger and hurt while asserting her loyalty and desire to be treated better.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: wants Bobby to be good to her and acknowledge her love
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: feels rejected but verbally stands her ground after Bobby remains unresponsive
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Bobby's emotional unavailability
evidence_quotes: Son of a bitch, Bobby! You quit pushin' me away like that! I've had enough a that to last me a lifetime!
Whyn't you just try an' be good to me for a change!?
There id'n anyone gonna look after you an' love you better'n I do, you know that.
Baby? Did you hear me?

relation_updates: expresses frustration in relationship with Bobby
status_updates: emotional state shifts to open anger and hurt

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rayette confronts Bobby about his behavior, fearing others' judgment; he reassures her, they reconcile with kisses, and he persuades her to go to Elton's, affirming their bond despite his vague response to her love.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette confronts Bobby about his dismissive behavior and fear of public judgment, leading to a tense exchange that ends in physical reconciliation after Bobby apologizes and kisses her.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seek emotional reassurance and respect in the relationship
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: temporarily reconciled but emotionally uncertain
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Bobby's lack of clear commitment and repeated emotional neglect
evidence_quotes: I'm not some piece a crap.
You treat me like I was.

relation_updates: Bobby apologizes but gives an ambiguous response to her declaration of love
emotional dependence reaffirmed despite ongoing instability
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I'm not some piece a crap

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Palm and Rayette's argument escalates as Bobby interrupts, shouting for everyone to be quiet, highlighting growing tension and conflict among the characters.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: defender of personal experience in argument
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette responds to Palm's criticism of consumerism and filth by referencing her own past struggles with clutter, using her lived experience to challenge the blanket condemnation of personal habits.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to defend her lifestyle choices
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rayette positions herself as someone who has personally dealt with excess and considered practical solutions, contrasting Palm's abstract and sweeping critique of consumer culture.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension between personal responsibility and systemic critique
evidence_quotes: Beside him, Rayette primps in the sunvisor mirror.

status_updates: enters direct ideological opposition to Palm
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I had so much garbage collectin' onto me every day, I was thinkin' about gettin a dispose all

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Palm and Rayette's argument escalates as Bobby interrupts, shouting for everyone to be quiet, highlighting growing tension and conflict among the characters.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: defender of her work experience and dignity
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette confronts Palm after being insulted, using her experience as a waitress to challenge Palm's dismissive view of the profession.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to defend her professional worth and respond to personal insult
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rayette asserts herself but is cut off when Bobby shouts for silence, ending the exchange without resolution.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: her defense is interrupted before she can fully respond, and Palm's disrespect remains unaddressed
evidence_quotes: Salesmen and cops are the ones. If you'd ever waitressed, honey, you'd know.
You better hold onto your tongue!
Don't you ever talk to me like that!
BOBBY Shut up! All of you!

relation_updates: fully antagonistic relationship with Palm Apodaca
auxiliary.persona_anchor: you better hold onto your tongue — I've worked hard as a waitress

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rayette compliments Catherine's hair, asks if it's natural, and requests ketchup, causing tension; Bobby storms off after being reprimanded, leaving an awkward silence as Rayette comments on his moodiness.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Bobby's partner, present at a formal family dinner
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette's comments about the property, Bobby's absence, and her request for ketchup disrupt the dinner's formality, highlighting her unfamiliarity with the family's social norms and triggering Bobby's angry departure.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to fit in and be accepted during the dinner
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: remains at the table after Bobby leaves, attempting to ease the tension by downplaying his behavior
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: lack of clarity from Bobby about their role in his family setting
evidence_quotes: This certainly is an improvement on the motel an' the coffee shop.
How could you have left such a beautiful place, Bobby?
Is there any ketchup around?
I guess Bobby's just about the moodiest man I ever ben with...

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I came here hoping not to intrude, just to be with him

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rayette pleads with Bobby to let her care for him, asserting no one else will love him as she does, but he remains unresponsive, deepening her emotional distress.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Rayette shifts from physical affection to verbal confrontation after Bobby pushes her away, asserting her unique devotion and demanding kindness, but receives no response.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to be loved and cared for in return
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rayette is emotionally rebuffed and left without acknowledgment, reinforcing her isolation in the relationship.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: her need for reciprocal love remains unmet.
evidence_quotes: Son of a bitch, Bobby! You quit pushin' me away like that! I've had enough a that to last me a lifetime!
Whyn't you just try an' be good to me for a change!?
There id'n anyone gonna look after you an' love you better'n I do, you know that.
Baby? Did you hear me? There is no response.

relation_updates: shifts from passive affection to active protest
auxiliary.persona_anchor: There id'n anyone gonna look after you an' love you better'n I do, you know that.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: The timeline traces 12 key developments across the character's screenplay trajectory.
